Decision: Use Rating Average as the prediction target.
Reson: Rating Average is the value the model will learn to predict.

Decision: Exculed ID, Name, BGG Rank, Users Rated, and Owned Users.
Reson: These columns are identifiers, text labels, ranking information, or post-release popularity signals that could cause leakage or memorisation.

Decision: Use selected board game design and attribute columns as numerical inputs.
Reson: These columns describe board game properties avaliable before or during game evaluation.

Decision: Use log-transformed versions of Max Players and Play Time.
Reson: Max Players and Play Time were heavily right-skewed, so log transformation reduces the influence of extreme values.

Decision: Create missing-value indicator columns for numerical features with missing values.
Reson: Missingness itself may contain useful information, wile actual missing values are imputed later.

Decision: Create binary indicator features for the 15 most common non-unknown mechanics.
Reson: Mechanics are multi-value text fields, so indicator features convert them into model-friendly numeric columns.

Decision: Create binary indicator features for all available non-unknown domains.
Reson: Domains are multi-value tetx fields and teher are only a small number of available domain categories.

Decision: Split the data into 80% training and 20% testing using random_state=42.
Reson: The test set is kept separate so future model performance can be evaluated on unseen data.

Decision: Fill missing feature values using median values learned from the training set only.
Reson: Learning imputation values from the training set only avoids leaking information from the test set.

Decision: Save prepared training/testing feature and target files, feature names, and imputation summary.
Reson: Saving prepared files allows future modelling notebooks to load the prepared data directly.
